sanskrit:
एषा ब्राह्मी स्थितिः पार्थ नैनां प्राप्य विमुह्यति।
स्थित्वाऽस्यामन्तकालेऽपि ब्रह्मनिर्वाणमृच्छति॥
transliteration:
eṣā brāhmī sthitiḥ pārtha naināṃ prāpya vimuhyati |
sthitvāsyām anta-kāle ’pi brahma-nirvāṇam ṛcchati ||
literal_meaning: This Brahman-grounded state, O Pārtha, having attained, one is not deluded; abiding in it, even at life’s end, one reaches Brahman-nirvāṇa.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna is promised that once the mind is anchored in a trans-personal, value-centric identity (brahmī sthiti), existential anxiety collapses; death becomes a doorway, not a threat.
psychological_state: Post-traumatic growth: after moral injury, the psyche re-orients from egoic survival to symbolic immortality through values bigger than the body.
trigger_scenario: A palliative-care patient who stops fighting death and instead spends final weeks mentoring younger patients, discovering a serene clarity that surprises the medical team.
anchor_text: Rest your mind in the changeless Self; then death is only a doorway.
